name: Rangda
description: Rangda is the demon queen of the Leyaks in Bali, Indonesia. Terrifying in appearance, she embodies darkness and destruction. Rangda is often depicted as an old widow, naked with long, unkempt hair, pendulous breasts, and long claws. Her face is horrifying, with fangs, a protruding tongue, and bulging eyes. She is a powerful sorceress who commands an army of evil spirits and is believed to be capable of causing disease, famine, and death. Rangda's origins are often linked to the historical Queen Mahendradatta, also known as Gunapriyadharmapatni, an 11th-century Javanese princess and queen consort of King Udayana of Bali. Legend has it that she was exiled by the king for practicing witchcraft, and in revenge, she became Rangda, the embodiment of evil. Her battles against Barong, the king of the spirits and symbol of good, are a central theme in Balinese mythology and are enacted in ritual dances. Rangda represents the negative aspects of the cosmos and the balance between good and evil, light and darkness. She is feared and revered in equal measure, as her power is undeniable, and her influence permeates Balinese spiritual beliefs.
subtitle: The Demon Queen of Bali

keyMyths.title: Rangda's Origin
keyMyths.description: Rangda is believed to be the transformation of Queen Mahendradatta after being exiled. She sought revenge through dark magic, becoming the powerful demon queen.

keyMyths.title: The Battle with Barong
keyMyths.description: Rangda is locked in an eternal struggle with Barong, the king of the spirits representing good. Their conflict symbolizes the balance between good and evil in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Rangda and Disease
keyMyths.description: Rangda is believed to be a bringer of disease and misfortune. Her power is feared, and rituals are performed to ward off her influence and protect communities.

sources.author: Adrian Vickers
